sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see what's happening! When I clicked on the red shape on the right:
1. The red shape lost its white border (the white outline was removed)
2. The red portion of the horizontal bar on the left (rows 30-33, cols 12-15) disappeared - the red and dark red parts were removed, leaving only the gray part
So clicking on a shape on the right removes that color from the left panel! This is a color-removal puzzle. The goal is probably to remove all colors from the left panel by clicking on the corresponding shapes on the right.
But wait, there are only 2 shapes on the right (red and blue), but there are multiple colors on the left (gray, red, dark red, blue, yellow, light green, sky blue, checkered pattern). Let me look more carefully at the left panel.
Looking at the left panel:
- A gray box with yellow dot inside (top area)
- A blue square next to it
- A horizontal bar: gray + red + dark red (middle)
- A checkered sky blue/dark gray pattern
- A gray box with light green dot (bottom)
After clicking the red shape, the red and dark red parts of the horizontal bar disappeared. So clicking removes ALL instances of that color family (red and dark red) from the left panel.
Let me now click on the blue shape to see if it removes blue things from the left.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

# Let me click the blue shape on the right to test this theory.
# The blue shape is around rows 34-40, cols 48-58
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 53}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
